mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly employment gains signal expanding corporate revenue streams, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid nominal rate hikes compress equity valuations by raising discount rates applied to future cash flows, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered instances despite rate hikes exceeding the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous industrial production growth and declining unemployment indicate supply-side capacity utilization aligning with labor demand, boosting short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero directional accuracy below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth in a tight labor market signals wage-driven consumption sustainability, supporting equity valuations over a monthly horizon.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with unemployment below 5.5 percent
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns when unemployment remains above 5.5 percent, or shows no statistical edge over a random baseline in late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained housing permit declines signal weakening consumer confidence and credit demand, depressing forward equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 500 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances following two consecutive months of permits below 500.

mechanism_narrative: Rising policy rates combined with stagnant real GDP growth indicate monetary tightening outpacing economic expansion, compressing equity multiples over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no predictive power for 60-day returns, or the signal yields positive returns in over 50% of historical stagflationary episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month industrial production growth reflects immediate manufacturing sector momentum, providing a positive signal for short-term equity price direction.
failure_condition: The rolling 5-day directional accuracy drops below 50% over any 12-month rolling window, indicating no persistent short-term momentum link.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer credit growth coincides with elevated inflation, real purchasing power erodes, leading to negative forward equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when credit growth exceeds its 12-month average despite inflation surpassing 2.5 percent.
